hero.title: The Day After
hero.reason: In honor of International Dance Day, The Day After is a film that portrays the importance of human connection and community, values that are also reflected in the universal language of dance.
hero.blurb: This powerful film depicts the aftermath of a nuclear disaster and serves as a reminder of the importance of human relationships. With its strong message and emotional impact, it's a must-watch.

released.title: Disturbia
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Disturbia is a gripping thriller that pays homage to Hitchcock's Rear Window, offering a fresh take on the classic voyeuristic theme.
released.blurb: This suspenseful film follows a teenager who becomes convinced that his neighbor is a serial killer, keeping viewers on the edge of their seats. With its clever plot twists and relatable characters, it's a great watch.

birthday.title: The Prophecy
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Daniel Day-Lewis, The Prophecy is an action-packed fantasy film that showcases his range and talent as an actor, even in a supporting role.
birthday.blurb: This dark fantasy film explores the battle between good and evil, with an all-star cast and impressive action sequences. Daniel Day-Lewis's performance adds depth to the story.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a mess of a thriller that fails to deliver on its intriguing premise.
awful.blurb: This convoluted film tries to pass off its nonsensical plot as clever, but ends up being a boring and confusing mess. With a talented cast wasted in underwritten roles, it's a shame this movie didn't live up to its potential.
